title: aragallus verticillaris
intro: Aragallus verticillaris, also known as White Prairie Clover, is a perennial legume native to the central United States. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is rich in isoflavones, which have been linked to various health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease and osteoporosis. Aragallus verticillaris can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in tincture form, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
